item.title: HSBC appoints David Rice as first chief AI officer
item.description: HSBC has appointed David Rice вҖӢas its first ever вҖҢChief AI Officer, the British bank said вҖӢon Monday, as вҖӢit seeks to cut costs вҒ by increasing the вҖӢuse of generative AI вҖӢtechnology across its businesses worldwide.

item.title: Fed's Goolsbee: Inflation now the greater risk, watching expectations closely - CNBC
item.description: Inflation is the greater risk facing the U.S. вҖӢeconomy right now with the вҖҢunemployment rate remaining fairly stable, Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee said on вҖӢMonday, though a quick вҖӢresolution of the Iran conflict could вҒ still leave the Fed poised вҖӢto cut rates later this вҖӢyear.

item.title: DoorDash rolls out emergency relief program as surging pump prices hit gig workers
item.description: Food delivery company DoorDash said on вҖӢMonday it is launching an вҖҢemergency relief program to help U.S. gig workers offset rising fuel costs.

item.title: Alibaba launches latest agentic AI platform with international unit's Accio Work
item.description: Alibaba has вҖӢpushed further into the global race for agentic artificial intelligence, with its вҖҢinternational commerce division launching Accio Work, a plugвҖ‘andвҖ‘play вҖңAI taskforceвҖқ it says can autonomously run complex business operations for small and medium-sized enterprises.

item.title: Nebius says "well-funded" for AI race after closing $4.3 billion debt raise
item.description: Nebius вҖӢclosed a $4.34 billion convertible debt funding round on Monday, with a вҖҢcompany executive saying that the European AI infrastructure firm is now "well-funded" to meet its 2026 capital spending plans of $16 billion to 20 billion.

item.title: OnlyFans owner Leonid Radvinsky dies of cancer at 43
item.description: Leonid Radvinsky, the owner of adultвҖ‘content вҖӢplatform OnlyFans, has died of cancer вҖҢat the age of 43, the company said in a statement on Monday.

item.title: China's open-source dominance threatens US AI lead, US advisory body warns
item.description: The dominance of China's open-source artificial intelligence is creating a "self-reinforcing competitive вҖӢadvantage", allowing it to challenge U.S. rivals despite restricted access to advanced AI chips, a U.S. congressional вҖҢadvisory body said on Monday.

item.title: BlackRock CEO Fink backs staying invested amid volatility, flags AI shift
item.description: Asset management giant BlackRock's CEO Larry Fink urged clients to stay invested despite ongoing market volatility, and highlighted that artificial intelligence is reshaping вҖӢinvestment strategies and the broader economy.

item.title: EU-Mercosur trade accord to apply provisionally from May 1
item.description: The European Union's free trade agreement вҖӢwith South American вҖҢbloc Mercosur will apply on a provisional basis from вҖӢMay 1, the вҖӢEuropean Commission said on вҒ Monday.

item.title: Instant view: World markets rally as Trump postpones military strikes on Iranian power plants
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump said on Monday the U.S. has had good and productive conversations with Iran and he will order the military to postpone any military strikes against Iranian power plants and energy infrastructure.

item.title: Oil falls over 13% on Trump postponing military strikes on Iran energy infrastructure
item.description: Oil вҖӢprices fell by вҖҢover 13% after вҖӢPresident Donald вҖӢTrump said he would вҒ order the вҖӢmilitary to postpone any вҖӢstrikes against Iranian power plants and energy вҖӢinfrastructure.

item.title: Dollar slides, stocks jump as Trump signals pause on Iran strikes
item.description: The dollar plunged and stocks surged on вҖӢMonday after U.S. President вҖҢDonald Trump said he had asked the Department of Defence вҖӢto postpone "any and all" military вҖӢstrikes against Iranian power plants вҒ and energy infrastructure for вҖӢfive days.

item.title: Dollar scarcity in Venezuela forces small firms to raise prices, turn to crypto
item.description: In central Venezuela, the owner of a mid-size pharmaceutical factory scrambles to obtain dollars to import chemicals and other ingredients for his headache and вҖӢfever remedies.

item.title: Victory Capital fires back at Peltz's Trian as Janus bidding war drags on
item.description: Victory Capital on Monday fired back at Nelson Peltz's Trian over the criticism вҖӢof its latest $8.6 billion proposal for asset manager Janus Henderson , вҖҢsaying the activist was making "efforts to blanket market with misinformation".

item.title: Morning Bid: Ticking time bomb
item.description: What matters in U.S. and global markets today

item.title: Exclusive: OpenAI sweetens private equity pitch amid enterprise turf war with Anthropic, sources say
item.description: ChatGPT maker OpenAI is offering private-equity firms a sweeter deal than rival Anthropic as both artificial intelligence companies court buyout firms to вҖӢform joint ventures aimed at raising fresh capital and accelerating adoption of enterprise AI products, according to people familiar with the talks.

item.title: US stock futures fall as escalating Iran conflict dims rate-cut hopes
item.description: U.S. stock index futures fell on Monday as the Middle вҖӢEast conflict intensified with energy infrastructure under threat, sending oil prices surging and pushing investors to вҖҢreprice U.S. interest rate cut expectations.

item.title: Exclusive: EU digital rules should apply to Big Tech's smart TVs, broadcasters tell antitrust chief
item.description: Google , Amazon , Apple and Samsung's smart TVs and virtual assistants вҖӢshould fall under the EUвҖҷs toughest tech rules because of their growing market power, the world's largest вҖҢbroadcasters told EU antitrust chief Teresa Ribera on Monday.

item.title: Hedge funds bet against U.S. stocks and turn to Europe, Goldman Sachs says
item.description: Hedge funds last week piled into bets against U.S. shares and emerging markets stocks in Asia, вҖӢwhile wagering that European shares would rise, said a вҖҢGoldman Sachs note to clients seen by Reuters on Monday.

item.title: Hopes give way to selling as investors brace for longer Middle East war
item.description: Investors losing hope for a quick resolution of the Middle East war are scrambling to cushion portfolios against prolonged strife and a bigger oil shock, as they seek shelter in cash and energy shares while cutting bonds and bets on tech and mining.

item.title: Italy fines review platform Trustpilot $4.6 million for misleading consumers; shares slip
item.description: Italy's competition regulator has fined online review platform Trustpilot вҖӢand its units 4 million euros ($4.6 вҖҢmillion) for failing to adequately verify the authenticity of reviews and for misleading consumers вҖӢabout how their services work.

item.title: Tokio Marine to form strategic partnership with Berkshire Hathaway, initially sell 2.49% stake
item.description: Tokio Marine Holdings Inc said on Monday it вҖӢwould form a strategic вҖҢpartnership with Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway by initially selling a 2.49% вҖӢstake through a third-party вҖӢallotment of treasury shares.

item.title: Japanese firms agree to 5.26% wage hike, top union group's preliminary data shows
item.description: Japanese companies have agreed to raise wages by more than 5% for a third consecutive year, early results from вҖӢannual labour talks showed on Monday, reflecting sustained gains in pay вҖҢthat policymakers see as key to fostering durable economic growth.

item.title: Goldman Sachs expects ECB rate hikes in April and June as inflation concerns mount
item.description: Goldman Sachs said on Monday it вҖӢexpects the European вҖҢCentral Bank to deliver two 25 basis point вҖӢinterest rate hikes вҖӢin April and June, joining вҒ peers J.P.Morgan and Barclays вҖӢas policymakers signal inflation вҖӢrisks driven by the Middle East war.

item.title: Finnair picks Embraer instead of Airbus for its narrow-body fleet renewal
item.description: Finnair said on Monday it will renew its European fleet with an order for 18 E195-E2 narrow-body вҖӢaircraft from Brazilian manufacturer Embraer , moving away from its вҖҢcurrent provider Airbus .

item.title: Toyota tender offer for Toyota Industries closes, results due Tuesday
item.description: Toyota's tender offer to take supplier Toyota Industries private at a price вҖӢof 20,600 yen per share was set to вҖҢclose on Monday, with the results expected at or after 3:30 p.m. (0630 GMT) on Tuesday.

item.title: STMicro starts STM32 microcontrollerВ deliveries in China
item.description: STMicroelectronics has started вҖӢdelivering its STM32 вҖҢwafers to customers in вҖӢChina вҖӢin collaboration with domestic вҒ producer вҖӢHuahong, the European вҖӢsemiconductor giant said on Monday.

item.title: Iron ore miners could face billions more in fuel costs due to Iran war, Fortescue says
item.description: Iron ore miners are at risk of incurring billions of dollars more in fuel costs if diesel вҖӢprices continue to rise, a senior executive at Australia's Fortescue said вҖҢon Monday.

item.title: Ping An investment arm questions US allocation
item.description: Insurer Ping An's , international arm is questioning further вҖӢU.S. investments, its CEO said, вҖҢciting the country's unreliability.

item.title: Focus: One battle after another: Iran war deals new blow to Europe's industrial heartland
item.description: In a spartan office adorned with plastic pots and detergent packaging at German chemical company Gechem, owner Martina Nighswonger feels she's running out of options.
